steps.title: Separate complete clauses
steps.content: The first complete clause says the observatory sites number nearly five dozen. The next clause says the radars track ionospheric irregularities. Those two ideas need a real sentence boundary, not just a comma.

steps.title: Choose the clean boundary
steps.content: Choices B and D leave a comma splice before the radars track. Choice C turns the location phrase into a relative clause and disrupts the main clause. The remaining option ends the first sentence and starts a grammatical participial opener. The answer is A.
